subject: [PATCH 3/4] fuse: update file mode when updating acls

reviews.author: Darrick Wong
reviews.summary:
Reviewer questioned whether the patch's changes are worth the effort required to implement them, referencing a previous discussion and suggesting that the benefits may not outweigh the costs.

Reviewer suggested rephrasing the sentence for clarity, specifically suggesting breaking it into its own sentence and providing an example of how to do so
sentiment_signals: questioning the value of the patch
reviews.raw_body:
I know, you said that last time ;)
https://lore.kernel.org/linux-fsdevel/CAJnrk1YL3KnON-WtNjNi+2GZ+6rYvnVUnYwCk5efv0o41XkxcA@mail.gmail.com/

and my reply is the same:
https://lore.kernel.org/linux-fsdevel/20260325222308.GH6202@frogsfrogsfrogs/#t

...which is to say, does anyone think that's worth the churn?

---

Thanks!

--D

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch updates the file mode when updating ACLs in a fuse filesystem, ensuring that sgid bits are correctly handled and updated.
